message:
chore(beads): update issue tracking metadata

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

@@ -3867,7 +3867,7 @@
 {"id":"ft-nu4.3.9.7","title":"CI e2e (extended): run approval allow-once scenario + keep artifacts","description":"# Task: CI extended e2e job

## Goal
Run the phase-4 "approval allow-once" E2E scenario in CI once the feature set is available.

This job specifically validates:
- RequireApproval UX payload
- wa approve command
- approval storage
- audited outcomes + redaction

## Behavior
- Execute:
  - ./scripts/e2e_test.sh --case approval-allow-once --verbose
- Always upload artifacts when failing.

## Registry
- Not a case. CI job extends the registry run with additional approval/allow-once scenarios.

## Logging
- Follow the standard E2E harness logging contract (`wa-4vx.10.6`) and runner (`wa-4vx.10.11`).
- Emit per-step start/end markers with timestamps + durations.
- Include case name and pane ids in every log line.
- Redact secrets (device codes, tokens) in logs and artifacts.

## Acceptance Criteria
- A CI failure includes enough artifacts to diagnose whether the bug is in:
  - policy decision
  - approval granting
  - retry behavior
  - audit/export


## Testing
- CI self-check:
  - Ensure the job logs include the artifacts upload location.
  - Consider a “known fail” dry run to verify artifact upload and summary formatting.
","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"JadeFox","created_at":"2026-01-18T10:42:15.120669036Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-02-08T10:35:33.668868614Z","closed_at":"2026-02-08T10:35:33.668804805Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-nu4.3.9.8","title":"CI setup job: run wa setup idempotency E2E (temp home, artifacts on failure)","description":"# Task: CI setup job (dotfile patching safety)

## Goal
Continuously validate that wa setup is safe and idempotent.

Dotfile patching is one of the fastest ways to lose user trust. This CI job ensures:
- dry-run does not write
- apply mode changes only the intended block
- re-running is a no-op

## Behavior
- Run the setup idempotency E2E:
  - ./scripts/e2e_setup_test.sh --all --verbose
  - or reuse ./scripts/e2e_test.sh if we unify harnesses

- Always upload artifacts on failure:
  - before/after copies
  - diffs
  - logs

## Dependencies
- Uses the E2E setup test described in wa-nu4.3.3.10.

## Registry
- Not a case. CI job focuses on setup idempotency cases from the registry.

## Logging
- Follow the standard E2E harness logging contract (`wa-4vx.10.6`) and runner (`wa-4vx.10.11`).
- Emit per-step start/end markers with timestamps + durations.
- Include case name and pane ids in every log line.
- Redact secrets (device codes, tokens) in logs and artifacts.

## Acceptance Criteria
- CI failures include all artifacts needed to debug the patcher step.


## Testing
- CI self-check:
  - Ensure the job asserts it is operating on a temp home directory and never touches `~`.
  - Include an intentional failure variant to verify artifact upload (diffs/before-after) works.
","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-18T10:47:15.226284812Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-29T17:39:39.982570062Z","closed_at":"2026-01-29T17:39:39.982447755Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-nu4.3.9.9","title":"Docs smoke tests: quickstart commands stay executable in CI","description":"# Task: Docs smoke tests (quickstart stays executable)

## Goal
Prevent docs drift by ensuring the README/quickstart instructions remain executable and safe.

This is a user-trust feature: if the quickstart breaks, new users churn.

## Scope
- Focus on commands that docs will recommend for a fresh install:
  - `wa --help`
  - `wa --version --verbose`
  - `wa robot quick-start`
  - `wa setup --dry-run` (in a temp HOME)
  - `wa doctor` (in a minimal environment; may be expected to warn but must be actionable)

- For commands that require a real WezTerm environment, docs should point to the E2E scripts as the canonical tested path rather than embedding fragile “just run this” snippets.

## Deliverables
1) A small docs-smoke runner (CI-friendly)
- Runs in a temp HOME + temp workspace.
- Captures stdout/stderr to artifacts.
- Never modifies real user dotfiles.

2) A mapping from “docs claims” → “test coverage”
- For each quickstart step, link to either:
  - a docs-smoke command, or
  - an E2E script case (e.g., `wa-4vx.10.7`, `wa-nu4.3.3.10`).

3) CI integration
- Add a CI job/step that runs docs smoke and uploads artifacts on failure.

## Logging & artifacts (must match the E2E philosophy)
- Artifacts directory contains:
  - `env.txt` (OS, rustc, cargo, enabled features)
  - `commands.txt` (exact commands executed)
  - `stdout.txt` / `stderr.txt` per command (or per step)
  - `summary.json` (pass/fail + durations)

- Logs must never contain secrets (even if users have real configs on their machine).

## Testing
- Unit-ish tests:
  - if docs smoke is implemented as a Rust subcommand, validate output schemas and deterministic ordering.
  - if implemented as a shell script, include at least one self-test that verifies it creates artifacts and returns correct exit codes.

- CI tests:
  - docs smoke runs on every PR and fails when a quickstart command regresses.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Docs smoke runs in CI and fails when a referenced quickstart command breaks.
- Failures are diagnosable from artifacts without rerunning locally.


## Testing
- “Tests for the tests”:
  - include one intentionally-failing step under a special flag (or a dedicated CI mode) to ensure artifact capture + summaries are working.
","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-18T17:42:24.357386135Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-02-07T05:35:06.753427576Z","closed_at":"2026-02-07T05:35:06.753360681Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
-{"id":"ft-nu4.4","title":"[EPIC] Phase 5: Advanced — vendoring, streaming, distributed mode, coordination","description":"# Phase 5 — Advanced Features

## Objective
Push wa from "solid local orchestrator" into "fleet-grade system":
- Selective WezTerm vendoring (feature-gated) for streaming output / lower overhead.
- Real-time output streaming (reduce polling and gaps).
- Optional distributed mode: `wa-agent` near mux servers + central aggregator.
- Multi-agent coordination helpers.

## Why this phase exists
The core system can ship without this. These features are justified only when they deliver measurable wins:
- latency, correctness (fewer gaps), or usability.

## Deliverables
- Vendored client prototype + benchmarks vs CLI.
- If ROI confirmed: ship `--features vendored` with strict version checks.
- Distributed protocol design + first implementation.

## Acceptance criteria
- Benchmarks show clear advantage for vendored/streaming modes.
- Distributed mode can ingest deltas from at least one remote domain without stalling the central watcher.



## Success Criteria
- Advanced capabilities (vendoring, streaming, distributed mode, coordination) remain optional and do not compromise core safety.
- Each advanced feature has clear boundaries, tests, and safe defaults.


## Testing
- Performance ROI validation:
  - Benchmarks must prove vendored/streaming modes are meaningfully better than CLI polling for target workloads.

- Safety/regression:
  - Optional features must compile-gate cleanly and have their own unit/integration tests.

- E2E (when distributed mode is implemented):
  - Distributed E2E tests must validate persistence, ordering, and secret-safe artifacts (`wa-nu4.4.3.5`).

## Acceptance Criteria
- All items in the following sections are satisfied: Success Criteria, Deliverables.
- All Testing requirements pass (unit/integration/E2E) with the logging/artifacts described above.
- Any explicit dependencies for this bead are implemented and validated via tests or E2E artifacts.

## Cross-references
- **wa-e34d9** (asupersync epic): Advanced features in this phase — especially streaming and distributed mode — depend heavily on a mature async runtime. The asupersync migration establishes the `tokio`-based async foundation that streaming subscriptions, agent-to-aggregator connections, and coordination protocols build upon. Ensure asupersync migration is complete (or at least the core async runtime layer is stable) before implementing streaming or distributed features. Backpressure, cancellation, and graceful shutdown patterns from asupersync should be reused here rather than reinvented.

## Test and Observability Plan
1. Add comprehensive unit tests covering happy path, boundary/edge cases, and explicit failure/error behavior for logic introduced or changed by this bead.
2. Add deterministic integration and end-to-end scenario scripts that exercise full workflow behavior, including at least one negative/failure scenario and one recovery scenario.
3. Emit detailed structured logs during tests and e2e runs (timestamp, subsystem, correlation_id, scenario_id, inputs, decisions, outcomes, reason_code/error_code).
4. Persist triage artifacts for failed runs (stdout/stderr, structured logs, snapshots/fixtures, and concise failure summaries) so regressions can be root-caused quickly.
5. Ensure test commands and evidence locations are documented in the bead implementation notes and wired into normal validation/CI paths.

## User Impact
1. Specify the concrete operator/agent workflow pain this bead removes (speed, reliability, safety, or clarity).
2. Define measurable user-facing outcomes (for example p95 latency, failure/timeout rate, time-to-recovery, or reduction in manual intervention steps).
3. Define no-regression UX guarantees and explicit rollback/degradation behavior if those outcomes are not met.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Deliver scoped distributed/streaming capability with deterministic behavior, explicit security/safety constraints, and no feature regression.
2. Require comprehensive validation: unit tests, integration tests, and end-to-end scripts for normal operations, partial failures, disconnect/retry paths, and recovery workflows.
3. Require structured diagnostics/logging for stream/session identities, sequencing, backpressure, persistence results, and failure taxonomy with reproducible artifacts.
4. Heavy validation/soak runs must use rch and include objective thresholds plus artifact bundles.
5. Closure requires auditable evidence and residual-risk ownership, including explicit notes for any blocked environmental prerequisites.","notes":"Depends on asupersync migration (ft-brc7d) for async runtime compatibility. Must connect to main dependency graph.","status":"in_progress","priority":3,"issue_type":"epic","assignee":"MagentaWillow","created_at":"2026-01-18T08:50:23.889729960Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-03-17T06:31:16.781274Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["e2e-tests","integration-tests","logging","quality-gate","sequencing","test-coverage","test-logging","user-impact"],"comments":[{"id":2376,"issue_id":"ft-nu4.4","author":"MagentaWillow","text":"2026-03-16 progress: taking a bounded distributed-listener sender-canonicalization fix in crates/frankenterm/src/main.rs. Root cause: handshake/ACL checks normalize agent identities, but replay keys, sender hashing, and persistence mapping were still using raw sender casing, which can fork one logical agent into multiple remote identities and let stale lower-seq metadata from a case-only variant overwrite newer state. Patch scope: canonicalize sender identity before replay guard/aggregator/persistence mapping; normalize distributed remote pane/domain/UUID helpers; add regressions for mixed-case sender pane mapping and listener ordering/persistence.","created_at":"2026-03-17T02:46:38Z"},{"id":2377,"issue_id":"ft-nu4.4","author":"MagentaWillow","text":"Progress: patched distributed sender canonicalization in crates/frankenterm/src/main.rs so case-only sender variants collapse onto one replay key / remote pane identity / sender sequence bucket. Static checks are green. First rch run failed remotely with dep-info os error 2 under /tmp target path; exact sender_case rerun is in flight on rch with project-local remote target dir and no local cargo fallback allowed.","created_at":"2026-03-17T02:56:29Z"},{"id":2378,"issue_id":"ft-nu4.4","author":"MagentaWillow","text":"Follow-up: remote sender_case rerun uncovered an earlier compile blocker in this same reserved file, unrelated to the distributed sender patch: load_session_show_pane_lookup() referenced session_restore::load_latest_checkpoint without the module in scope. Fixed by qualifying it as frankenterm_core::session_restore::load_latest_checkpoint. rustfmt --check and git diff --check are green again. Fresh rch sender_case rerun is active on a cold worker and still compiling the workspace.","created_at":"2026-03-17T02:59:39Z"},{"id":2381,"issue_id":"ft-nu4.4","author":"RubyCanyon","text":"RubyCanyon fresh-eyes audit is on a separate non-conflicting path: investigating  lifecycle/shutdown semantics only. No  overlap.","created_at":"2026-03-17T03:30:55Z"},{"id":2383,"issue_id":"ft-nu4.4","author":"MagentaWillow","text":"Progress: found and fixed a second distributed identity bug in crates/frankenterm/src/main.rs. When a remote client omitted session_id, the listener fell back to raw handshake.agent_id, so mixed-case reconnects could still split replay-session keys and bypass stale-seq protection across connections. Added distributed_resolve_session_id(), a unit regression for normalized agent fallback, and a cross-connection listener regression that expects dist.replay_detected on the stale mixed-case reconnect. Local rustfmt/diff hygiene are green. Targeted rch fallback run on vmi1152480 failed with the recurring dep-info os error 2 under /data/projects/frankenterm/target/rch-magenta-sender-case, with no source diagnostics and no local cargo fallback allowed.","created_at":"2026-03-17T03:36:45Z"},{"id":2384,"issue_id":"ft-nu4.4","author":"MagentaWillow","text":"Progress: patched the next distributed ordering seam in crates/frankenterm/src/main.rs. Replay protection was session-scoped, but aggregator dedupe and pane_seq_by_sender were still sender-scoped, so a legitimate new remote session for the same sender could not cleanly restart at seq 1. Current fix introduces a session-scoped internal sender key for dedupe/order tracking while keeping persisted remote pane identity keyed to the canonical sender. Added listener regression distributed_listener_accepts_seq_reset_for_new_session_scope plus unit coverage for distributed_sender_session_scope normalization. Local rustfmt/diff hygiene are green; targeted rch session_scope validation is running on vmi1153651 with no local cargo fallback.","created_at":"2026-03-17T03:43:13Z"},{"id":2385,"issue_id":"ft-nu4.4","author":"MagentaWillow","text":"Continuing the distributed-mode hardening slice in crates/frankenterm/src/main.rs after bv robot triage kept higher-impact items blocked or actively owned. Latest root cause: replay validation was session-aware, but ordering/dedupe buckets were still sender-scoped, so a legitimate new session for the same sender could restart at seq 1 yet still be treated as stale by local ordering state. Patch now uses a sender+session internal ordering scope while preserving persisted pane identity on canonical sender. Local rustfmt/diff hygiene are green; targeted rch session_scope validation is running remotely on vmi1153651 with no local cargo fallback allowed.","created_at":"2026-03-17T03:45:48Z"},{"id":2386,"issue_id":"ft-nu4.4","author":"MagentaWillow","text":"Fresh-eyes follow-up found an additional edge case in the new sender+session ordering helper: appending the session hash could push the internal sender key past wire_protocol::MAX_SENDER_ID_LEN when the original sender already sat near the 128-byte cap. Fixed by truncating the canonical sender prefix to preserve the hashed session suffix within the protocol limit, and expanded the helper unit test to cover the max-length case. Re-running local formatting/hygiene and then redoing the targeted remote session_scope validation on the updated code.","created_at":"2026-03-17T03:48:20Z"},{"id":2389,"issue_id":"ft-nu4.4","author":"MagentaWillow","text":"Fresh-eyes review found a resource-lifecycle bug in the new session-scoped ordering fix: aggregator sender state and pane_seq_by_sender entries were now keyed by sender+session, but unlike the old sender-scoped path they were never torn down on disconnect. That could accumulate per-session state across reconnect churn until stale pruning or capacity limits hit. Fixed by adding explicit per-connection scope registration/reference counting and teardown cleanup, plus core/main regressions proving cleanup only happens on the last active reference.","created_at":"2026-03-17T03:57:59Z"},{"id":2395,"issue_id":"ft-nu4.4","author":"MagentaWillow","text":"2026-03-17 progress: continuing distributed hardening in crates/frankenterm/src/main.rs and crates/frankenterm-core/tests/distributed_streaming_e2e.rs after bv kept higher-impact lanes blocked/owned. Fresh-eyes fixes landed: (1) inferred remote seq gaps now encode explicit bounds before calling storage, so an initial PaneDelta with seq > 0 no longer drops its leading gap when no prior segment exists; added regressions in main.rs and distributed_streaming_e2e.rs. (2) listener now removes replay_guard state when aggregator admission fails with TooManyAgents, so a capacity rejection does not permanently poison retry of the first seq for a new session; added a focused regression in main.rs. Local rustfmt --edition 2024 and git diff --check are green. rch validation is running for distributed_streaming_e2e_initial_seq_gap_is_persisted_before_first_delta and distributed_capacity_reject_does_not_poison_replay_retry; no local cargo fallback allowed.","created_at":"2026-03-17T06:02:15Z"},{"id":2398,"issue_id":"ft-nu4.4","author":"jemanuel","text":"2026-03-17 progress: normalized stale distributed_streaming_e2e first-segment fixtures to the live seq=0 contract. Happy path now starts 0,1 and asserts no synthesized gaps / no pane gap repairs; robustness flow now starts at 0 and aligns explicit gap + pane_seq_plan; invalid-sender recovery now starts the recovered sender at seq 0. Local rustfmt and git diff --check are green. Focused rch distributed_streaming_e2e test run is active; no source diagnostics yet.","created_at":"2026-03-17T06:25:17Z"},{"id":2399,"issue_id":"ft-nu4.4","author":"jemanuel","text":"2026-03-17 validation: offloaded rch run passed for crates/frankenterm-core/tests/distributed_streaming_e2e.rs after the seq=0 fixture normalization. Command: cargo test -p frankenterm-core --test distributed_streaming_e2e distributed_streaming_e2e_ -- --nocapture. Worker vmi1293453. Result: 10 passed, 0 failed. Only diagnostics were the known unrelated unused-import warnings in crates/frankenterm-core/src/mcp.rs.","created_at":"2026-03-17T06:31:16Z"}]}
+{"id":"ft-nu4.4","title":"[EPIC] Phase 5: Advanced — vendoring, streaming, distributed mode, coordination","description":"# Phase 5 — Advanced Features

## Objective
Push wa from "solid local orchestrator" into "fleet-grade system":
- Selective WezTerm vendoring (feature-gated) for streaming output / lower overhead.
- Real-time output streaming (reduce polling and gaps).
- Optional distributed mode: `wa-agent` near mux servers + central aggregator.
- Multi-agent coordination helpers.

## Why this phase exists
The core system can ship without this. These features are justified only when they deliver measurable wins:
- latency, correctness (fewer gaps), or usability.

## Deliverables
- Vendored client prototype + benchmarks vs CLI.
- If ROI confirmed: ship `--features vendored` with strict version checks.
- Distributed protocol design + first implementation.

## Acceptance criteria
- Benchmarks show clear advantage for vendored/streaming modes.
- Distributed mode can ingest deltas from at least one remote domain without stalling the central watcher.



## Success Criteria
- Advanced capabilities (vendoring, streaming, distributed mode, coordination) remain optional and do not compromise core safety.
- Each advanced feature has clear boundaries, tests, and safe defaults.


## Testing
- Performance ROI validation:
  - Benchmarks must prove vendored/streaming modes are meaningfully better than CLI polling for target workloads.

- Safety/regression:
  - Optional features must compile-gate cleanly and have their own unit/integration tests.

- E2E (when distributed mode is implemented):
  - Distributed E2E tests must validate persistence, ordering, and secret-safe artifacts (`wa-nu4.4.3.5`).

## Acceptance Criteria
- All items in the following sections are satisfied: Success Criteria, Deliverables.
- All Testing requirements pass (unit/integration/E2E) with the logging/artifacts described above.
- Any explicit dependencies for this bead are implemented and validated via tests or E2E artifacts.

## Cross-references
- **wa-e34d9** (asupersync epic): Advanced features in this phase — especially streaming and distributed mode — depend heavily on a mature async runtime. The asupersync migration establishes the `tokio`-based async foundation that streaming subscriptions, agent-to-aggregator connections, and coordination protocols build upon. Ensure asupersync migration is complete (or at least the core async runtime layer is stable) before implementing streaming or distributed features. Backpressure, cancellation, and graceful shutdown patterns from asupersync should be reused here rather than reinvented.

## Test and Observability Plan
1. Add comprehensive unit tests covering happy path, boundary/edge cases, and explicit failure/error behavior for logic introduced or changed by this bead.
2. Add deterministic integration and end-to-end scenario scripts that exercise full workflow behavior, including at least one negative/failure scenario and one recovery scenario.
3. Emit detailed structured logs during tests and e2e runs (timestamp, subsystem, correlation_id, scenario_id, inputs, decisions, outcomes, reason_code/error_code).
4. Persist triage artifacts for failed runs (stdout/stderr, structured logs, snapshots/fixtures, and concise failure summaries) so regressions can be root-caused quickly.
5. Ensure test commands and evidence locations are documented in the bead implementation notes and wired into normal validation/CI paths.

## User Impact
1. Specify the concrete operator/agent workflow pain this bead removes (speed, reliability, safety, or clarity).
2. Define measurable user-facing outcomes (for example p95 latency, failure/timeout rate, time-to-recovery, or reduction in manual intervention steps).
3. Define no-regression UX guarantees and explicit rollback/degradation behavior if those outcomes are not met.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Deliver scoped distributed/streaming capability with deterministic behavior, explicit security/safety constraints, and no feature regression.

That could accumulate per-session state across reconnect churn until stale pruning or capacity limits hit. Fixed by adding explicit per-connection scope registration/reference counting and teardown cleanup, plus core/main regressions proving cleanup only happens on the last active reference.","created_at":"2026-03-17T03:57:59Z"},{"id":2395,"issue_id":"ft-nu4.4","author":"MagentaWillow","text":"2026-03-17 progress: continuing distributed hardening in crates/frankenterm/src/main.rs and crates/frankenterm-core/tests/distributed_streaming_e2e.rs after bv kept higher-impact lanes blocked/owned. Fresh-eyes fixes landed: (1) inferred remote seq gaps now encode explicit bounds before calling storage, so an initial PaneDelta with seq > 0 no longer drops its leading gap when no prior segment exists; added regressions in main.rs and distributed_streaming_e2e.rs. (2) listener now removes replay_guard state when aggregator admission fails with TooManyAgents, so a capacity rejection does not permanently poison retry of the first seq for a new session; added a focused regression in main.rs. Local rustfmt --edition 2024 and git diff --check are green. rch validation is running for distributed_streaming_e2e_initial_seq_gap_is_persisted_before_first_delta and distributed_capacity_reject_does_not_poison_replay_retry; no local cargo fallback allowed.","created_at":"2026-03-17T06:02:15Z"},{"id":2398,"issue_id":"ft-nu4.4","author":"jemanuel","text":"2026-03-17 progress: normalized stale distributed_streaming_e2e first-segment fixtures to the live seq=0 contract. Happy path now starts 0,1 and asserts no synthesized gaps / no pane gap repairs; robustness flow now starts at 0 and aligns explicit gap + pane_seq_plan; invalid-sender recovery now starts the recovered sender at seq 0. Local rustfmt and git diff --check are green. Focused rch distributed_streaming_e2e test run is active; no source diagnostics yet.","created_at":"2026-03-17T06:25:17Z"},{"id":2399,"issue_id":"ft-nu4.4","author":"jemanuel","text":"2026-03-17 validation: offloaded rch run passed for crates/frankenterm-core/tests/distributed_streaming_e2e.rs after the seq=0 fixture normalization. Command: cargo test -p frankenterm-core --test distributed_streaming_e2e distributed_streaming_e2e_ -- --nocapture. Worker vmi1293453. Result: 10 passed, 0 failed. Only diagnostics were the known unrelated unused-import warnings in crates/frankenterm-core/src/mcp.rs.","created_at":"2026-03-17T06:31:16Z"},{"id":2402,"issue_id":"ft-nu4.4","author":"jemanuel","text":"2026-03-17 progress: took another bounded distributed fix after bv kept graph-top work blocked. Root cause: listener advanced replay_guard, aggregator sender seq, and pane_seq_by_sender before durable distributed persistence completed, so a storage failure could poison retries and drop the payload. Patch adds snapshot/restore rollback for replay + aggregator admission state, restores pane sequence tracking on failed delta/gap persistence, and adds focused regressions for failed delta retryability and failed gap tracker rollback. Local rustfmt and git diff --check are green; targeted rch validation for distributed_failed_ tests is running.","created_at":"2026-03-18T00:38:07Z"}]}
 {"id":"ft-nu4.4.1","title":"[EPIC] Selective WezTerm vendoring (feature vendored) + version compatibility","description":"# [EPIC] Selective WezTerm vendoring

## Mission
Unlock capabilities not available via `wezterm cli`, while preserving a CLI-first default:
- zero-copy scrollback access
- real-time output streaming (enables true subscription vs polling)

## Default posture
- CLI-first remains the foundation.
- Vendoring is an **optional acceleration lane** behind `--features vendored`.

## Key risks
- Upstream API churn
- Version compatibility problems
- Larger binary / more complex build

## Definition of done
- `--features vendored` builds successfully.
- wa can detect local WezTerm version compatibility and surface it in `wa doctor`.
- Unified client picks best available backend (vendored when compatible, otherwise CLI).


## Success Criteria
- Vendored WezTerm components are minimal, clearly version-pinned, and do not break upstream compatibility expectations.
- CI validates vendor build and ensures core features still work without vendoring enabled.


## Testing
- Feature-gate tests:
  - Ensure `--features vendored` builds and unit tests run, and `--no-default-features` (or default) still works without pulling vendored deps.

- Compatibility tests:
  - Fixture-based tests for version/commit matching logic (supported/unsupported versions).
  - `wa doctor` should surface compatibility deterministically.

- RPC smoke tests:
  - Basic mux RPC framing tests with fixtures so failures are diagnosable without a live WezTerm.

## Acceptance Criteria
- All items in the following sections are satisfied: Success Criteria, Definition of Done.
- All Testing requirements pass (unit/integration/E2E) with the logging/artifacts described above.
- Any explicit dependencies for this bead are implemented and validated via tests or E2E artifacts.

 {"id":"ft-nu4.4.1.1","title":"Vendored feature scaffolding: add wezterm crates + compile behind feature flag","description":"# Task: Vendored feature scaffolding

## Goal
Add the minimal dependency + feature wiring to compile vendored WezTerm crates behind `--features vendored`.

## Deliverables
- Cargo feature `vendored`.
- Optional dependencies on the selected WezTerm crates (selective, not full fork).
- A build that succeeds in both modes:
  - default (CLI-only)
  - vendored

## Testing
- Build/test matrix:
  - `cargo check --all-targets` (default)
  - `cargo check --all-targets --features vendored`
  - ideally `cargo test --features vendored` once minimal unit tests exist

- Ensure feature gating is strict:
  - enabling `vendored` must not pull heavy deps into default builds

## Acceptance Criteria
- `cargo build` works without vendored deps.
- `cargo build --features vendored` works and does not affect default behavior.

 {"id":"ft-nu4.4.1.2","title":"DirectMuxClient: connect to mux socket + basic RPC framing (vendored)","description":"# Task: DirectMuxClient (vendored)

## Goal
Implement a direct mux client that can connect to the WezTerm mux socket and perform RPCs using vendored protocol types.

This is the foundation for vendored scrollback reads and real-time subscriptions.

## Socket discovery
- Discover mux socket path via:
  - WezTerm defaults
  - wa config override (must exist for unusual setups)
- Support multiple platforms (macOS/Linux) as needed.

## Connection + framing
- Implement:
  - connect with timeout
  - request/response framing with bounded reads
  - graceful close

## Robustness
- Never allow unbounded reads from the socket.
- Return structured errors that can be surfaced in `wa doctor` and `wa status`.
- Treat protocol decode errors as “incompatible version” signals (and fall back to CLI where appropriate).

## Testing strategy
- Offline unit tests:
  - frame encode/decode round-trips
  - truncated/invalid frames handled without panics

- Optional manual smoke:
  - on a machine with a running mux server, connect and perform a simple request (e.g., list panes)

## Acceptance Criteria
- In vendored mode, wa can connect to a running mux server and perform a simple request (e.g., list panes).
- Decode errors are handled gracefully and do not panic.
